page: Microsoft Azure D2a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 51 GB of local temporary storage. No GPU resources are included.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance are more limited. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively, reflecting the dual-thread single-core physical layout. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds categorized as weak, and small-block cached reads scoring in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D2a_v4 offers a low-cost entry point for dedicated x86_64 compute resources on Azure. The primary tradeoff is the limited multi-threaded scaling and lower memory bandwidth, which restricts its utility for memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads.

This server type is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads that rely on single-core performance rather than massive parallel processing. Appropriate use cases include basic web serving, lightweight background tasks, single-threaded scripting, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 51 GB of local storage. While single-core CPU benchmarks show strong performance, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, placing in the lower percentiles. This resource profile makes the D2a_v4 a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require heavy parallelization or high memory throughput. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded scripting, and basic development environments.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual machine with dedicated AMD EPYC single-core strength and balanced memory for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
Features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory
Includes 51 GB of local temporary storage
Strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
